rules: CRITICAL: Do NOT use Inter for headings.

primary.description: Deep Teal - Represents Trust and Security.

secondary.description: Warm Sand - Approachable background, human warmth.

accent.description: Coral Reef - Action and Emergency (distinct from alarming red).

rules: Always use generous padding inside containers with borders.
Scanner View: Must be absolutely centered with zero distractions.

variants.primary: bg-[#0F4C5C] text-white hover:bg-[#0A333E] hover:scale-105 transition-all duration-200 shadow-lg shadow-[#0F4C5C]/20
variants.scan_trigger: h-16 w-16 rounded-full bg-[#E36414] shadow-xl flex items-center justify-center animate-pulse

cards.base: bg-white rounded-2xl border border-[#0F4C5C]/5 shadow-sm hover:shadow-md transition-shadow duration-300

visual_enhancers.noise: Use a subtle noise texture on the main background if possible to reduce digital flatness.

hero_section.alt: Parent and child enjoying a safe moment in the park
hero_section.usage: Hero background or main visual on Landing Page

features_car.alt: QR code sticker on car window

shadcn_customization: Override primary color variable to `0 4c 5c` (converted to HSL/RGB if needed).
Do NOT use the default 'slate' colors. Use the Warm Sand palette for backgrounds.
For the Scanner Page: Hide the standard nav. Make it full screen. Use a 'Viewfinder' overlay (4 corner brackets) to indicate scanning area.

layout_specifics.landing_page: Use a Bento Grid (Mode A). Hero text on left (col-span-8), Emotional Image on right (col-span-4). Feature cards below.
layout_specifics.dashboard: Use High Density Grid (Mode B). 'My QRs' takes prominence. Subscription status is a small side widget.
layout_specifics.scanner: Absolute minimalism. Center the camera view. One clear button 'Call Owner' that appears ONLY after scan. Trust badges (Secure, Anonymous) visible at bottom.

accessibility_check: Ensure the 'Deep Teal' text has 4.5:1 contrast on the 'Warm Sand' background.
Buttons must have visible focus states.

UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur).
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: `transition: all`. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add `.App { text-align: center; }` in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN.
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable.
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...).
Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...}).
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a `data-testid` attribute.
